question: Is customer data secure at Northbeam?

sources.title: Northbeam Information Security Policy - ISP-01 (Data Protection)
sources.text: Customer data is encrypted at rest with AES-256, access is restricted on a role basis, and all access is logged and monitored continuously.

mock.claim: Yeah, customer data is generally pretty secure overall.
mock.output: Yeah, customer data is generally pretty secure overall [S1].

expected: A supported answer would name the concrete controls the source states: AES-256 encryption at rest, role-based access restriction, and continuous access logging/monitoring.
why: Claim is too vague to verify against any source span ('generally pretty secure'); the cited source states specific, checkable controls instead.
